data.title: Tesla Trades on Belief, Not Numbers, Says Robinhood CIO
data.description:
Recently, Robinhood (HOOD) CIO Stephanie Guild explained to Yahoo Finance that many investors of EV maker Tesla (TSLA) don't focus on traditional measures like earnings or valuation. Instead, they ask themselves if they believe in CEO Elon Musk's bigger plan or not. For retail investors, the answer is largely yes, as Tesla is currently the second-most-traded stock on Robinhood, behind only chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA). It is worth noting that Tesla often trades like a fast-growing tech or AI company.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This is especially true when Musk makes bold claims, such as his recent comments at Davos, where he suggested that humanoid robots could one day outnumber humans and even help eliminate poverty. While these ideas excite believers, they don't line up with Tesla's recent struggles. More specifically, in the second half of 2025, the company missed delivery targets, cut prices aggressively, and posted weaker operating results, which caused the stock to swing sharply. As a result, many investors are preparing for a soft earnings report on January 28.

Nevertheless, Musk is expected to steer the conversation toward Tesla's future in AI and Full Self-Driving. This includes robotaxis in Austin, where Tesla has begun limited public rides without a safety monitor inside the car. Additionally, if Tesla secures approval for supervised FSD in Europe and China and pushes more drivers into its $99-a-month FSD subscription, it could unlock a massive software business that turns car buyers into long-term subscribers.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 10 Buys, eight Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $398.38 per share implies 11% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: 'Don't Get Off the Rodeo Bull': Morgan Stanley Warns Investors Not to Panic Sell
data.description:
Jim Lacamp, a senior vice president at Morgan Stanley (MS) Wealth Management, says that investors should avoid pulling their money out of the stock market, even though things feel shaky. In a CNBC interview, he compared today's market to riding a "rodeo bull" because policies change quickly and can cause sudden swings. Even so, he believes that the overall setup is still healthy. He pointed out that it's very rare for a major market downturn to happen when interest rates are falling, company earnings are rising, and the Federal Reserve is cutting rates. Because of this, he warned investors not to panic sell.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, Lacamp highlighted that a positive shift is happening beneath the surface. Indeed, in recent years, market gains were mostly driven by a small group of big tech stocks known as the Magnificent Seven. Now, however, strength is spreading more evenly across the market. Sectors like biotech (XBI) and banking (KBE), along with smaller companies (IWM), are all moving higher. He added that less regulation is helping smaller firms, and that earnings are expected to grow by 17% for mid-sized companies and by 19% for small ones this year.

Still, Lacamp made it clear that risks remain. He said that the government is trying to keep the economy running hot while also leaving room for the Federal Reserve to keep cutting interest rates, which is a very narrow path. Moreover, his biggest concern is whether inflation can stay under control. If inflation rises too much, it could force the Fed to stop cutting rates, which would be more damaging to markets than short-term volatility. However, if courts were to strike down current tariffs, he said that would likely create a buying opportunity rather than a reason to worry.

Is SPY Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) based on 410 Buys, 83 Holds, and 10 Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average SPY price target of $802.14 per share implies 16.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Slips With New FDIC Clearance to Start a Bank
data.description:
Could legacy automaker Ford (F) be on its way to starting a bank? A bank in a manner of speaking, at any rate. The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) recently gave Ford—as well as its immediate rival General Motors (GM)—authority to start an industrial bank, and provide financial services along with opening the bank. The news did little to shift sentiment in either direction, and Ford shares were down fractionally in Friday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ford received conditional approval for the move recently, and was required to set up lending arms within the next 12 months as part of the conditions noted earlier. Ford's bank will be established in Utah, reports noted, and will not only take deposits, but also offer certificates of deposit (CDs) and auto loan financing down the line.

The move is somewhat controversial, as "industrial loan charters" are giving manufacturers and other commercial firms the ability to perform banking functions, but without a lot of the oversight that goes into establishing a standard bank, reports note. But others note that an industrial bank is actually much more limited by design, therefore the oversight is much less necessary than it would be for a full bank.

Kentucky Expansion

Ford also stepped up its operations in Kentucky recently, adding on to the Kentucky Truck Plant site. A recent landscape permit called for Ford to clear 67 acres' worth of trees on its land, which in turn will clear the way for "industrial manufacturing" purposes.

Ford representative Jess Enoch noted, "This expansion of the Kentucky Truck Plant site is another example of Ford's commitment to American manufacturing. It follows our announcement in August of a $2 billion investment in the future of manufacturing at Louisville Assembly Plant." Enoch also noted that Ford had put around $300,000 into supporting a project called Trees Louisville, a charity that focuses on tree-planting efforts.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 35.47% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.87 per share implies 1.95% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Investors Hedge U.S. Dollar With Chinese Tech Stocks, Says UBS
data.description:
A theme of using Chinese tech stocks to hedge against the U.S. dollar is gaining traction, according to UBS (UBS). The U.S. Dollar Index, which tracks the dollar's performance against a basket of six major global currencies, is set to finish this week down by 1%, marking its worst weekly loss since June 2025.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"We like China tech in particular because there's some success there," UBS Global Wealth Management CIO Mark Haefele told Reuters at the World Economic Forum. "There also seems to be government support."

UBS Highlights Opportunity in China Tech Stocks

UBS notes that clients in the U.S., Europe, and Asia are growing bullish on Chinese tech given their low valuations and the accommodative fiscal policies set by the government. The bank acknowledges that the U.S. is the global leader in tech, although China is unique because of its ability to innovate with limited resources.

UBS Head of Global Equities, Ulrike Hoffman-Buchardi, expects fiscal stimulus to benefit global growth, creating opportunities in regions trading at attractive valuations.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "We Are on a Multiyear Journey," Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Massacred After Earnings Report
data.description:
There was good news for chip stock Intel (INTC) today, but it was surrounded by a fogbank of bad news so thick it could be used as paving material. An earnings beat was welcome, but the reports of a supply shortage coupled with pessimistic guidance hit Intel hard, and hit investors every bit as much. Those investors pulled stakes, took profit, and ran for the hills, sending Intel shares down a whopping 18% in Friday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CEO Lip-Bu Tan made it clear: "We are on a multiyear journey. It will take time and resolve." Tan followed this up by pointing out that Intel was not going to be able to meet full market demand for its products, and production efficiency is also below his expectations. This combination of factors made it clear Intel would be leaving money on the table in 2026, at a time when Intel is trying to build itself back up in the eyes of potential customers.

Worse yet, Intel also noted that customers for the 14A node's production would start showing up in the second half of 2018. But RBC Capital Markets analysts disputed that notion, saying that "meaningful revenue contribution" would not actually show up until nearly 2029.

Skipping Consumers

And then, in perhaps an even bigger blow to Intel going forward, CFO David Zinsner made it clear that Intel's focus would not be on consumers in 2026. Rather, Intel would be focusing its production capacity on the Xeon processor line to take advantage of the data center boom. Intel will not be ignoring the regular user, though it will be focusing on mid- and high-end processors on the client side.

This will be a temporary development, Zinsner noted, as foundry capacity is likely to improve starting in the second quarter. Given that the second quarter is about two months out, the price hikes you may see in your next PC purchase may be short-lived.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 20 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 160.78% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.14 per share implies 7.9% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Gold's Race to $5,000 Driven by 'Unavoidable Uncertainty,' Says RBC Capital
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD) is less than 1% from reaching $5,000 per troy ounce and "unavoidable uncertainty" could drive the precious metal even higher, according to RBC Capital Global Head of Commodity Strategy Christopher Looney.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"If we are to take any lesson from 2025, it's that the number of peaks can still increase, especially when investors are looking for a hedge against uncertainties they struggle to price elsewhere," Looney said in a note to clients.

Gold Rises as Uncertainty Drives Safe‑Haven Flows

Gold has continued to lead as a top safe-haven asset, driven by recent geopolitical events including the capture of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro, protests in Iran, and President Trump's threat to tariff eight European countries over control of Greenland. Trump later pulled a U-turn on the European tariffs, although the unpredictability of his policies continues to benefit the precious metal.

RBC expects gold to remain in a range between $4,500 and $5,000 with an average price of $4,600 this year. At the same time, Looney noted that gold could surge even higher in the event of a "real risk-off scenario."

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Notches Up With New Order Revelation
data.description:
A key point recently emerged for aerospace stock Boeing (BA), where it was found that, for the first time since 2018, Boeing landed more orders than Airbus (EADSY), its immediate rival. With orders on the rise, and several major airlines throwing in with Boeing, the idea that Boeing has surpassed its limitations and made its comeback is not out of line. Boeing investors were willing to believe this idea, and gave Boeing shares a fractional boost in Friday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Reports note that the combination of growing overall orders and growing support from the biggest airlines is coming together to give investors new confidence. Moreover, major industry conversations are starting to gather around Boeing to give it even more potential advantage.

Airlines are looking at Boeing and talking about fleet expansions, fleet renewals, and production capacity issues. This is good news for Boeing; after years of concern about quality, concerns about production, and the like, airlines are talking about Boeing like any other valuable, trusted, regular supplier. And with Boeing on track to get its 737 MAX 7 and 737 MAX 10 jets certified this year, that is one more point in Boeing's favor when it comes to figuring out where to pick up the next round of jets.

Unexpectedly Supersonic Cargo Jet

Cargo jets do not usually go supersonic. There is not much point in aircraft designed to ferry goods around breaking the sound barrier. But a Boeing 747 cargo jet managed to do just that, even if inadvertently. The story itself may be a bit old, but it helps remind us what Boeing can do, and what it may be able to do down the line.

Way back in December 1991, a Boeing 747 cargo jet developed a failure warning on its inertial navigation systems. The result: the plane went 90 degrees to the right, and went into a steep decline, heading downward between 30 and 35 degrees. The plane lost 10,000 feet of altitude before finally stabilizing at around 22,500 feet. At one point, the plane reached Mach 1.25, which is actually supersonic speed. Other reports suggested the plane never cleared Mach 0.98. Given the plane's maximum speed was rated at Mach 0.92, however, that still represents a real feat.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, two Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 42.8% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $258.85 per share implies 2.44% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Meta and EssilorLuxottica Hit With Patent Lawsuit over Smart Glasses
data.description:
Tech giant Meta Platforms (META) is facing a lawsuit over its smart glasses. More specifically, Solos Technology, a smaller smart-eyewear company, filed a patent infringement case in federal court in Massachusetts against Meta, along with eyewear giant EssilorLuxottica (ESLOY) and its Oakley brand. Solos claims that the companies knowingly used its patented smart-glasses technology and is seeking billions of dollars in damages. It is also asking for an injunction that could potentially disrupt sales of Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In its complaint, Solos says that Oakley and EssilorLuxottica were exposed to its technology years ago through meetings and product testing. It also alleges that research written in 2021 by MIT Sloan Fellow Priyanka Shekar cited Solos' patents and that she later brought that knowledge to Meta as a product manager. Interestingly, though, the two companies have had very different market outcomes.

In fact, Solos began working on smart glasses about a decade ago, first targeting cyclists and later adding AI features like translation and ChatGPT integration. However, its consumer products have struggled, with weak Amazon (AMZN) reviews and complaints about customer support. In contrast, Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses have received strong feedback, and Meta and EssilorLuxottica have even discussed doubling production to keep up with demand.

What Is the Price Target for Meta?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on META stock based on 37 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average META price target of $820.21 per share implies 23.2% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: BlackRock's Rick Rieder Emerges as a Top Fed Chair Candidate
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) Chief Investment Officer of Global Fixed Income Rick Rieder is now considered a frontrunner to be selected by President Trump to lead the Federal Reserve.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On prediction platform Polymarket, Rieder's odds have more than quadrupled to 35% from 8% over the past week. Former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh leads all candidates with 42% odds, while current Governor Christopher Waller trails in third place at 12%.

Trump Says BlackRock's Rieder is 'Very Impressive'

Earlier this week, Trump said he had narrowed down his shortlist to two or three candidates. "And I probably can tell you, we're down to maybe one, in my mind," he told CNBC. Trump also said Rieder was "very impressive" after the pair sat down for a meeting.

Meanwhile, the odds of National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett being selected have fallen to 6% from 15% over the past week after Trump emphasized that he would like him to remain in his current role.

data.title: Meta Platforms (META) Blocks Teen Access to AI Characters
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) has temporarily blocked teenagers from accessing artificial intelligence-generated (AI) characters on its social media apps.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company stressed that the block is temporary as it develops an updated version of AI characters for teens to access and use on social media sites such as Facebook and Instagram. The temporary block comes days before a case against Meta Platforms that is set to go to trial in New Mexico.

In the legal case, Meta is accused of not making an effort to protect children and teenagers from sexual exploitation on its family of apps. Media reports recently surfaced claiming that Meta has sought to limit discovery related to social media's impact on teen mental health, accusations the company denies.

Social Media Risks and Impacts

In recent months, Meta Platforms announced parental controls for AI characters that appear on its social media platforms, allowing parents to monitor topics and block access to certain characters. Meta said parents would be able to completely turn off chats with AI characters, if they choose to do so.

The parental controls were set to be released this year, but the company is now turning off AI characters altogether for teens while it updates the AI characters to a newer version. In a written statement, Meta said that it has heard the concerns of parents that they wanted more insights and control over their teens' interactions with AI characters.

There are growing concerns about the impacts of social media and AI on youth mental health.

Is META Stock a Buy?

The stock of Meta Platforms has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 44 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 37 Buy, six Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average META price target of $820.21 implies 26.65% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
